title: Lab-01 — HTML-first Interactive Game (minimal JS)
task: Complete the Lab 1 document. Build an HTML-first, multi-page interactive game. Use JavaScript only for the small browser interactions required by the lab.
acceptance: Upload the completed files to labs/lab-01/ with index.html as the entry point.
Include the pages, navigation, media, and interactive behavior required by the Lab 1 document.
Add a link from the student portfolio to the published lab and verify that it loads through GitHub Pages.
Add labs/lab-01/README.md with run instructions and a brief reflection on the completed work.

title: Lab-02 — CSS: Crash Course
task: Complete the Lab 2 document, a crash course in pure CSS without frameworks.
acceptance: Upload the completed files to labs/lab-02/ with index.html as the entry point.
Demonstrate the selectors, typography, spacing, sizing, backgrounds, layout, and responsive behavior required by the Lab 2 document.
Add a link from the student portfolio to the published lab and verify that its pages and assets load correctly.
Add labs/lab-02/README.md with run instructions and a brief reflection on the completed work.

title: Lab-04 — JavaScript Basics: Hi–Lo Game (MVP → MVC)
task: Build the Hi–Lo guessing game by following the Lab 4 document, moving from a minimum viable implementation toward the required MVC structure.
acceptance: Add all files required by the Lab 4 document under labs/lab-04/ and preserve the required structure and filenames.
Implement the required game logic, DOM updates, event handling, and timed interactions.
Run the application in the browser and resolve unintended console errors.
Add labs/lab-04/README.md with run instructions, what you built, challenges, and possible improvements.

title: Lab-05 — JavaScript OOP + Phaser: Dodger Game
task: Build the Phaser Dodger game by following the Lab 5 document and use object-oriented JavaScript and Phaser scenes to organize the application.
acceptance: Add all files required by the Lab 5 document under labs/lab-05/.
Use JavaScript classes or object-oriented patterns and Phaser scenes to organize the application.
Implement the movement, enemies, collisions, scoring, and other features required by the Lab 5 document.
Add labs/lab-05/README.md with run instructions, what you built, challenges, and possible improvements.

title: Lab-06 — REST Client: Quiz Game + Leaderboard (Modules + Fetch)
task: Build the browser-based quiz and leaderboard client described in the Lab 6 document. Use Fetch for the required HTTP requests, organize the application with ES modules, and manage its client-side state.
acceptance: Upload the completed files to labs/lab-06/ with index.html as the browser entry point and run the application through an HTTP development server.
Use ES modules to separate application responsibilities.
Use Fetch with promises or async/await for the required quiz and leaderboard requests.
Display appropriate loading, empty, success, and error states.
Add labs/lab-06/README.md with run instructions, endpoint information, and a brief reflection.

title: Homework 2 — Thick-Client Browser Application Capstone
task: Design, build, deploy, and demonstrate a substantial thick-client browser application that integrates the JavaScript and React concepts developed throughout the course.
acceptance: Create a separate project repository containing the capstone source, documentation, and reproducible setup instructions.
Create projects/project-02/README.md in the student portfolio with links to the project repository and deployed application.
Build a substantial browser interface with organized components, meaningful application state, and multiple user interactions.
Use React and Vite unless an alternate client-side architecture is approved by the instructor.
Include asynchronous data access or browser persistence where appropriate, with clear loading, empty, and error behavior.
Provide a responsive interface, final project documentation, and a working deployment for the Day 18 demonstration.
Add the repository URL, deployment URL, and demonstration notes to the project-02 Issue and mark it ready for review.

title: Additional React Labs — Planned, Not Yet Assigned
task: Additional React labs will be published during Days 14–17. Their final numbers, roadmap keys, titles, requirements, acceptance criteria, and due dates have not yet been assigned.
